steps.title: Pressure in Fluids
steps.content:
Pressure in a fluid (liquid or gas) acts in **all directions** and increases with depth.

Pressure due to a column of fluid:

P = hρg

Where: h = depth (m), ρ = density of fluid (kg/m³), g = gravitational field strength (N/kg)

Atmospheric pressure is about **101 000 Pa** (101 kPa) at sea level.

steps.title: Pressure in Fluids
steps.question: What happens to the pressure in a liquid as depth increases?
options: Pressure stays the same regardless of depth
Pressure only increases if the liquid is moving
steps.correctFeedback: Correct! P = hρg — pressure is directly proportional to depth. The greater the depth, the more fluid above pushing down.
steps.misconceptionFeedback: Pressure increases with depth because more fluid is above you. P = hρg — a greater h means greater P. This is why deep-sea submarines need reinforced hulls.
